doom.driver: Oil briefly hits $119 and Europe gas prices surge after attacks on energy facilities in Qatar, Iran.
doom.blurb: THE GAS FIELDS ARE BURNING NOW
doom.summary: Israel punched Iran's South Pars gas field, Iran punched Qatar's LNG complex back, and now oil is flirting with $119 a barrel while Europe watches its heating bill catch fire. Canada is frantically drafting G7 de-escalation principles, which is the diplomatic equivalent of bringing a fire extinguisher to a volcano. The war has officially graduated from 'oil export disruption' to 'the entire Gulf energy architecture is a target board.'

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Israel-Iran conflict has expanded to mutual strikes on gas infrastructure, with Qatar's LNG complex now hit and Gulf states warning their 'defensive stance won't last forever.'

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Canada's G7 de-escalation push and Japan PM's 'difficult' Trump talks signal allied coordination is strained and reactive rather than proactive.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Pentagon AI planning and Sony's 135,000 deepfake removals are background noise compared to kinetic escalation dominating the week.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Trump threatening to 'blow up' South Pars if Qatar strikes continue introduces unpredictable escalation triggers into an already volatile energy war.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: No significant AI alignment developments today; the existential drama is firmly analog and hydrocarbon-flavored.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, extreme close-up filling the entire frame with the frost-rimmed base of a colossal spherical LNG storage tank — the curve of its white-painted steel hull dominating the composition like a frozen moon, condensation ice crystals rendered in precise dithered pixels catching the last of the light, the tank's lower hemisphere glowing blood orange and volcanic amber from reflected inferno light sources just off-frame, the upper curve dissolving into absolute black sky, no antialiasing, the concrete plinth at the tank's equator cracked and stained with rust-brown streaks, a thick insulated pipeline entering frame from the lower-right corner — silver lagging tape wrapped in segments, elevated on a squat concrete saddle, the pipe's surface acting as a mirror reflecting a smeared streak of orange fire-glow and black petrochemical smoke that fills the reflected world entirely, the Ras Laffan waterfront implied only through a sliver of dark blue-green Persian Gulf water visible at the extreme lower edge of the frame, a chaotic flare stack flame barely intruding from the top-left corner as a single tongue of dithered orange-red pixels eating into the black sky, the entire image color-graded to blood orange and deep volcanic black with cool tones nearly absent, 16-bit SNES pixel palette, no text, no people, no weapons firing, tension communicated entirely through industrial scale and the consuming fire-reflected light.

dawn.driver: Mathematician wins 2026 Abel prize for solving 60-year-old mystery. Gerd Faltings shocked mathematicians around the world for his 1983 proof of the Mordell conjecture, which brought together seemingly disparate mathematical fields
dawn.summary: While energy infrastructure burns across the Middle East and markets spiral, the Abel Prize reminds us that the human intellect keeps reaching toward permanence. The Mordell conjecture — a problem that stumped the world's best mathematical minds for six decades — fell to one person's focused brilliance, weaving together fields that seemed unrelated until they suddenly weren't. In a week of cascading crises, a proof that will outlast every war quietly takes its place in the canon of human achievement.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: The Abel Prize recognizes a landmark proof connecting disparate branches of mathematics, a reminder that cooperative intellectual traditions transcend geopolitical chaos.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Canada is actively drafting G7 and Middle East de-escalation principles, showing multilateral diplomacy is still being attempted even as the war widens.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Kenyan grandmothers formed a soccer team to mentor teenagers, outrunning the next generation on the field while building community resilience.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: New Scientist reports the Iran war's energy shock could accelerate the clean energy transition as nations scramble to reduce fossil fuel dependency.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Google's AI rerouted over 100 transatlantic flights to reduce climate-warming contrails, a quiet but meaningful win for AI applied to environmental good.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Silhouette composition, strong backlight, dramatic golden-yellow light blazing through tall arched windows of a high-ceilinged European academic office at the Max Planck Institute in Bonn, warm fresh daylight flooding the room and casting everything into deep shadow at the foreground. The environment dominates — floor-to-ceiling blackboards lining the walls glow faintly with backlit chalk equations, donut-torus shapes and multi-holed geometric surfaces rendered in sequence across the board, the Pythagorean equation cascading among them, all silhouetted against the blazing window light. A single figure, the mathematician, stands in stark black silhouette at the blackboard, chalk raised, back half-turned, utterly absorbed, his form haloed by the golden-yellow morning light pouring in behind him. On a desk in the foreground, also silhouetted, sits an absurdly thin stack of papers — the 18-page proof — beside dense academic volumes, its slimness striking against the thick tomes. Clustered in a doorway, small silhouetted figures of colleagues lean together in stunned reaction. A faded portrait hangs on the far wall, ghostly in the warm haze. Palette: golden yellow, warm amber, fresh green accents in the chalk glow, deep charcoal shadows. Confident, steady warmth.
